name: Aisling Sans
classification_notes: Described by the foundry as combining modern structural precision with subtle humanist influence and open proportions, placing it in the humanist sans-serif tradition despite geometric tendencies.
summary: A ten-weight contemporary sans with humanist leanings, designed for branding, editorial systems and interface typography across print and digital.
description: Built on open proportions and controlled spacing, Aisling Sans aims for a calm typographic voice that sits between geometric precision and humanist warmth. The ten-weight family spans Thin to Black with 430 glyphs and coverage across 221 Latin languages, making it practical for multilingual identity and publication work. It is positioned for environments where legibility and tonal restraint matter more than expressive personality.
foundry_blurb: S6 Foundry describe Aisling Sans as a contemporary sans-serif shaped by clarity, softness and visual restraint, with open proportions and carefully balanced geometry. They note its combination of modern structural precision with subtle humanist influence, and position it for identity systems, publication design, interface typography and contemporary brand environments where clarity and tone need to coexist naturally.

name: Alskar Sans
classification_notes: The foundry explicitly describes Alskar Sans as having a strong geometric structure and clean proportions, placing it firmly in the geometric sans-serif tradition, with softened details that prevent it from feeling purely mechanical.
summary: A geometric sans-serif with clean proportions and open counters, built to serve branding, editorial and digital systems across a broad range of scales.
description: Alskar Sans is structured around geometric construction tempered by softened transitions and carefully moderated spacing, preventing the coldness that can undermine purely mechanical sans designs. Five weights from Light to Black, with an Extended width variant, give the family enough range for both display and text settings. Broad Latin language coverage spanning over 200 languages makes it a practical choice for identity work that must travel across linguistic contexts.
foundry_blurb: S6 Foundry describe Alskar Sans as a contemporary sans-serif built around strong geometric structure and clear typographic rhythm. They note that softened transitions and open counters balance precision with warmth, and that the family performs consistently across scales, feeling bold and architectural in large compositions while maintaining readability at smaller sizes. It is positioned as a versatile foundation for modern identity systems requiring clarity, flexibility and long-term consistency, with multilingual support for over 200 Latin languages.

name: Abaliss Sans
classification_notes: Wide proportions, open counters, curved terminals and softened geometry place this in the humanist sans tradition, though the modernist structure and calm rhythm give it a more neutral, contemporary character.
summary: A wide-proportioned sans with open counters and curved terminals, designed for editorial, branding and interface work across print and digital.
description: Built around generous spacing and a calm visual rhythm, it draws on modernist structure while introducing softer detailing to avoid clinical precision. Five weights run from Light to Bold with variable font capability and OpenType features, covering 221 Latin languages across 720 glyphs. The result is a controlled, legible family suited to both running text and larger typographic compositions.
foundry_blurb: S6 Foundry describe Abaliss Sans as a contemporary sans-serif built around balance, openness and typographic control, with wide proportions, open counters and curved terminals lending warmth without sacrificing precision. Designed for editorial, branding, interface and long-form communication, it supports 221 Latin languages, includes OpenType features and is available as a variable font.

name: Ariom
classification_notes: Described explicitly as a geometric sans-serif with architectural influence, vertical emphasis, and sharp structure, distinguishing it from neo-grotesque systems.
summary: A five-weight geometric sans-serif with sharp, architecturally informed letterforms and broad Latin language coverage aimed at branding and poster work.
description: Ariom is built on a strict geometric skeleton with vertical stress and compact proportions, drawing from modernist typography and architectural forms. Optical corrections temper the rigidity so the typeface reads cleanly without feeling mechanical. Five weights from Light to Bold serve branding systems, posters, motion graphics and digital environments that call for a constructed, assertive tone.
foundry_blurb: S6 Foundry describe Ariom as a geometric sans-serif with sharp structure, compact rhythm, and a strong contemporary presence, influenced by modernist typography and architectural forms. They position it for branding systems, visual identities, posters, motion graphics and digital applications, noting that its clean geometry and vertical emphasis give it a pronounced graphic character at larger sizes, while subtle optical corrections keep it from feeling cold or mechanical.

name: Aprex Mono
classification_notes: A monospaced typeface with geometric detailing and open forms, designed explicitly for code, interfaces and editorial work, placing it firmly in the mono category with a contemporary geometric character.
summary: A contemporary monospaced family in five weights designed to move beyond coding aesthetics towards branding, editorial and interface use.
description: Built on classic monospace proportions, Aprex Mono introduces subtle geometric detailing, open letterforms and moderated contrast to produce a texture that reads as technical without feeling sterile. Five weights from Light to Bold, variable font capability and OpenType features give it practical range across code editors, motion graphics and identity work. Coverage extends to 221 Latin languages across 720 glyphs.
foundry_blurb: S6 Foundry describe Aprex Mono as a contemporary monospaced typeface designed for clarity, precision and structured visual rhythm, with carefully balanced proportions and refined spacing that take it beyond traditional coding aesthetics to support branding, editorial design, interfaces and modern digital communication.

name: Aprex Sans
classification_notes: The page describes rational construction with subtle humanist influence, open counters and carefully shaped terminals, placing it firmly in the humanist sans tradition despite its geometric leanings.
summary: A contemporary sans with humanist warmth built for branding, editorial and interface work across a broad Latin language range.
description: Aprex Sans pairs rational construction with subtle humanist influence, using open counters, controlled geometry and carefully shaped terminals to stay legible across sizes and contexts. Six weights run from Light to Bold with variable font support, and 750 glyphs cover 221 Latin-script languages. The family is positioned as a workhorse system for identity, UI, packaging and publication rather than a purely expressive display face.
foundry_blurb: S6 Foundry describe Aprex Sans as a contemporary sans-serif that balances precision, flexibility and visual clarity for modern communication systems. Built with open proportions and refined spacing, it suits branding, editorial, interfaces and digital environments, and ships with a broad weight range, multilingual support, stylistic alternates and variable font capability.

name: Arlo Sans
classification_notes: Described as a clean, neutral sans-serif with open forms and restrained detailing, positioning it firmly in the grotesque tradition rather than geometric or humanist camps.
summary: A neutral, open-formed grotesque in five weights, built for editorial systems, interface design and corporate communication.
description: Arlo Sans prioritises legibility and calm reading rhythm through smooth curves, stable proportions and generous spacing. The family avoids strong personality in favour of consistency across headlines, body text and interface typography, making it a practical choice for brand systems that demand restraint. With coverage across 210 Latin languages and 470 glyphs, it is a workmanlike option for multilingual projects.
foundry_blurb: S6 Foundry describe Arlo Sans as a clean and highly functional sans-serif designed around simplicity, balance and everyday usability. Built with open forms and restrained detailing, it delivers a neutral typographic voice that remains contemporary without feeling anonymous, and is suited to editorial systems, interface design, corporate communication and identity work where consistency and legibility are essential.

name: Bake Sans
classification_notes: Rounded terminals, balanced proportions and controlled geometric construction place this squarely in the geometric sans tradition, softened toward a friendlier, more humanist register.
summary: A rounded geometric sans with soft terminals and relaxed proportions, aimed at branding, packaging and interface work where warmth is required without sacrificing legibility.
description: Five weights from Light to Bold cover a wide range of applications, from editorial and packaging to digital interfaces, with 555 glyphs across 207 Latin languages. The rounded forms and gently shaped terminals give it a conversational character that separates it from colder geometric models, while disciplined spacing keeps it consistent across print and screen. A practical choice for hospitality, lifestyle and consumer-facing brands that need approachable typography with a contemporary finish.
foundry_blurb: S6 Foundry describe Bake Sans as a contemporary sans-serif built from rounded forms and balanced proportions, designed to feel approachable, modern and visually warm without sacrificing clarity. They note that its gentle curves and terminals give it a more human and conversational character than traditional geometric sans-serifs, while controlled spacing ensures consistency across print and digital environments. The family is intended for branding, packaging, editorial, hospitality, lifestyle and interface applications.

name: Babel Sans
classification_notes: The foundry describes controlled geometry and balanced proportions as central to the design, placing it within the geometric sans tradition, though subtle curves and terminal details push it toward a contemporary humanist-inflected geometric rather than a strict geometric.
summary: A nine-weight geometric sans built around balanced proportions and controlled geometry, pitched at identity systems, editorial layouts and digital interfaces.
description: Subtle details in the curves, terminals and internal spacing give Babel Sans a degree of visual rhythm that keeps it from slipping into pure neutrality. The weight range runs from Thin to Black and the family covers 221 Latin-script languages across 720 glyphs with OpenType features. It sits comfortably in branding and communication design where a clean, structured typographic voice is needed across multiple formats and environments.
foundry_blurb: S6 Foundry describe Babel Sans as a contemporary sans-serif built from balanced proportions and controlled geometry, designed to deliver a clean, structured and adaptable typographic voice. They note that subtle character in the curves, terminals and spacing creates a stronger visual rhythm and a more recognisable presence without sacrificing usability, making it suited to identity systems, editorial layouts, digital interfaces and large-scale communication.

name: Bellamy Sans
classification_notes: Described as combining modern geometric influence with softer humanist characteristics, placing it at the intersection of geometric and humanist sans-serif traditions rather than purely in either camp.
summary: A geometric-humanist sans with soft curves and restrained detailing, aimed at branding, editorial and interface work.
description: Bellamy Sans sits between geometric structure and humanist warmth, using smooth curves and controlled spacing to produce a calm typographic tone. Five weights from Light to Bold cover the range expected of a workhorse branding or editorial family, with 575 glyphs and coverage across 221 Latin-script languages. The design avoids sharp rigidity through subtle terminal detailing, keeping it readable across print, screen and packaging contexts.
foundry_blurb: S6 Foundry describe Bellamy Sans as a refined contemporary sans-serif built around smooth curves, careful spacing and balanced proportions. They position it as combining modern geometric influence with softer humanist characteristics, suited to identity systems, publication design, interface typography and packaging, with an emphasis on readability and visual refinement across both print and screen.

name: Faffin Sans
classification_notes: Described as balancing geometric precision with softer detailing, compact proportions and clean structural forms place it firmly in the geometric sans tradition, with a contemporary graphic character.
summary: A compact geometric sans with tight spacing and a confident graphic presence, suited to branding, posters and editorial work.
description: Faffin Sans runs across five weights with matching italics, built around controlled proportions and a dense typographic texture that reads well at larger sizes. The design pairs clean structural geometry with subtle quirks that introduce personality without disrupting legibility. Wide language coverage across over 200 Latin-script languages makes it a practical choice for international projects.
foundry_blurb: S6 Foundry describe Faffin Sans as a contemporary sans-serif with bold proportions, compact rhythm and a strong graphic presence. They note it combines clean structural forms with subtle quirks, balancing geometric precision with softer detailing to feel modern and expressive rather than purely functional. The family is positioned for branding, posters, editorial design, packaging and digital communication, with over 500 glyphs and support for 207 Latin languages.

name: Florid Sans
classification_notes: Florid Sans combines modern structural clarity with organic, softened letterforms and open spacing, placing it closer to humanist sans-serifs than rigid geometric constructions.
summary: A contemporary sans-serif with softened curves and open spacing, pitched at branding, editorial and lifestyle design across print and digital.
description: Florid Sans draws on organic shaping and fluid proportions to sit between the warmth of a humanist sans and the cleanliness of a geometric one. Five weights from Light to Bold cover a broad range of typographic needs, with 760 glyphs and support for 211 Latin languages making it a practical choice for international projects. Its understated character suits packaging, interface design and editorial systems where a refined but approachable tone is required.
foundry_blurb: S6 Foundry describe Florid Sans as a contemporary sans-serif designed with fluid proportions, open spacing and a calm visual rhythm. They highlight its subtle curvature as a departure from rigid geometric sans-serifs, allowing the typeface to maintain readability and warmth while keeping a clean, modern appearance. The family is positioned for editorial systems, branding, lifestyle communication, packaging and interface design.

name: Lang Sans
classification_notes: Described as built around geometric construction with disciplined proportions and compact spacing, placing it firmly in the geometric sans category, with optical moderation preventing it from reading as purely mechanical.
summary: A four-weight geometric sans built on disciplined proportions and compact spacing, aimed at branding, editorial and digital interface work.
description: Clean geometry and controlled optical adjustments give Lang Sans a structured, direct character without tipping into stiffness. Subtle shaping at the curves and terminals adds just enough distinction to separate it from purely mechanical grotesques. Four weights from Light to Bold cover a broad range of typographic tasks, and the family includes over 500 glyphs across more than 200 Latin languages.
foundry_blurb: S6 Foundry describe Lang Sans as a contemporary sans-serif combining geometric construction with carefully moderated optical adjustments to achieve a modern visual tone that feels direct, confident and highly structured. They position it for branding systems, editorial layouts, digital interfaces, signage and visual identities where typography must communicate precision and consistency with a contemporary edge.

name: Lunema Sans
classification_notes: Described as a contemporary sans-serif with geometric proportions and inktrap detailing; the clean modern structure and controlled geometry place it firmly in the geometric sans category with a technical inflection.
summary: A geometric sans-serif with controlled inktrap detailing across five weights, designed for branding, editorial and interface applications.
description: Sharp internal cuts and inktrap-inspired shaping give the letterforms a degree of engineered tension that becomes more legible at display sizes, without disrupting clarity at text scales. Five weights from Light to Bold each carry matching italics, and the family covers over 200 Latin languages across 450 glyphs with OpenType support. The balance between clean geometric proportions and subtle structural detail makes it serviceable across both functional UI and expressive branding contexts.
foundry_blurb: S6 Foundry describe Lunema Sans as a contemporary sans-serif defined by sharp internal cuts, controlled inktraps and refined geometric balance. They note that the inktrap-inspired shaping introduces tension and rhythm within letterforms, giving the typeface a stronger visual identity without compromising clarity. Designed for branding, editorial systems, interfaces and digital communication, the family is intended to feel precise, elegant and highly adaptable across modern communication environments.

name: Lagom Grotesk
classification_notes: The foundry explicitly describes it as a contemporary grotesk, built around disciplined proportions, controlled spacing and functional simplicity, with no excessive stylisation.
summary: A disciplined contemporary grotesque spanning five weights, designed for branding, editorial systems and screen interfaces where legibility must not compete with content.
description: Built around controlled spacing and balanced proportions, Lagom Grotesk aims for a neutral but refined voice that reads consistently across headlines, running text and UI environments. Subtle optical adjustments across the five weights produce a stable typographic texture suited to both print and screen. The family covers 206 Latin languages across 560 glyphs, making it a practical choice for multilingual identity and communication projects.
foundry_blurb: S6 Foundry describe Lagom Grotesk as a restrained contemporary grotesk designed around balance, clarity and functional simplicity. They note its disciplined proportions and controlled spacing deliver a neutral yet refined typographic voice suited to modern branding, editorial systems and interface design, with subtle optical adjustments producing a stable, readable texture across print and screen.

name: Neues Grotesque
classification_notes: Described by the foundry as a contemporary grotesk drawing influence from classic European grotesks, with disciplined proportions and restrained detailing consistent with the grotesque sans-serif tradition.
summary: A contemporary grotesque with restrained detailing and systematic proportions, offered in eight weights with matching italics and broad Latin language coverage.
description: Drawing from classic European grotesque conventions, this family prioritises consistency and optical balance over expressive character. Eight weights from Thin to Black each have a companion italic, giving designers sufficient range for identity systems, editorial layouts and interface typography. With 740 glyphs covering 218 Latin-script languages, it is a competent utility grotesque suited to multilingual publishing and corporate communication.
foundry_blurb: S6 Foundry describe Neues Grotesque as a contemporary grotesk built around neutrality, precision and systematic clarity. The family is intended to function across branding, editorial systems, interfaces and corporate communication, with disciplined proportions and refined spacing informed by classic European grotesks. They position it as a functional, dependable typographic system offering strong readability and long-term flexibility across print and digital media.

name: Rotary Sans
classification_notes: Classified as a rounded sans-serif with geometric construction; soft curves and balanced proportions place it firmly in the geometric sans tradition with a rounded variant sensibility.
summary: A rounded geometric sans in five weights, built for branding, packaging and interface work where warmth and legibility need to coexist.
description: Soft curves and controlled spacing give Rotary Sans a calm, approachable quality without tipping into playfulness. The five-weight family runs from Light to Bold, covering the range needed for editorial systems, product packaging and digital interfaces. Latin coverage extends across 221 languages, making it a practical choice for international branding projects.
foundry_blurb: S6 Foundry describe Rotary Sans as a contemporary sans-serif built from rounded geometry, smooth rhythm and balanced proportions, designed to feel modern and approachable across print and digital environments. They position it for branding, packaging, editorial systems and interface design where typography needs to be friendly and refined without becoming overly playful.

name: Neues Haus
classification_notes: Explicitly described as a geometric sans-serif shaped by architectural structure and modular rhythm, with influences from functional European design systems and modernist clarity.
summary: A geometric sans-serif drawing on European modernist design traditions, built for branding, signage and editorial applications requiring typographic discipline.
description: Constructed around strong proportions and disciplined geometry, the family introduces optical corrections and balanced spacing to soften the rigidity typical of purely geometric sans-serifs. Five weights from Light to Bold cover a wide range of applications, from large-scale signage to body text, with coverage across 221 Latin languages. It sits comfortably in cultural communication, branding systems and digital environments where order and visual precision are priorities.
foundry_blurb: S6 Foundry describe Neues Haus as a contemporary geometric sans-serif shaped by architectural structure, modular rhythm and modernist clarity. They note that it draws influence from functional European design systems while introducing subtle refinements that reduce the rigidity often found in geometric sans-serifs, with careful spacing and optical corrections allowing it to perform across branding, signage, editorial layouts, cultural communication and digital environments.

name: Saffron
classification_notes: Saffron is described as an expressive display typeface with bold contrast, sculpted curves and exaggerated proportions, placing it firmly in the display serif category with high-contrast detailing aimed at luxury and fashion contexts.
summary: A high-contrast display serif with sculpted curves and exaggerated proportions, aimed at luxury branding, fashion and editorial work.
description: Saffron balances soft curves against sharp details to produce a rich visual rhythm that reads best at large scales. Five weights from Light to Bold each carry matching italics, giving designers enough range for editorial hierarchies without losing the typeface's dramatic character. The family covers 220 Latin languages across 790 glyphs, making it broadly usable across multilingual luxury and cultural projects.
foundry_blurb: S6 Foundry describe Saffron as an expressive display typeface built on bold contrast, sculpted curves and a strong contemporary personality. They position it for luxury branding, beauty, fashion, editorial and packaging work where typography needs to function as a defining element of visual identity, noting that exaggerated proportions and carefully controlled contrast give it a sophisticated yet expressive character.

name: RNA Sans
classification_notes: RNA Sans is described as built on modular structure and sharp geometry with angular transitions and controlled spacing, placing it squarely in the geometric sans-serif tradition with a technical, systematic character.
summary: A modular geometric sans built for interfaces and digital systems, with angular transitions and open counters across six weights.
description: RNA Sans is constructed around sharp geometry and systematic spacing, giving it a precise, engineered quality suited to technology branding and interface design. Angular transitions and open counters keep the letterforms functional without tipping into mechanical sterility. The family covers 221 Latin languages across 450 glyphs with OpenType features, making it a practical choice for international digital products.
foundry_blurb: S6 Foundry describe RNA Sans as a contemporary sans-serif shaped by modular structure, technical precision and compact visual rhythm. They position it for modern branding, interfaces, motion graphics and information-driven environments where clarity and consistency are essential, noting that systematic construction is balanced with subtle detailing to introduce movement and distinction within the letterforms.

name: Vertical Sans
classification_notes: A condensed sans-serif with tall proportions and disciplined spacing, fitting the grotesque tradition through its structural sharpness and compressed construction designed for high vertical emphasis.
summary: A three-weight condensed sans-serif built around tall proportions and strong vertical rhythm for headlines, branding and signage work.
description: Tall proportions and controlled internal spacing give this condensed sans-serif a clear structural presence without sacrificing legibility at either large or functional scales. Sharp forms and disciplined counters produce a contemporary tone suited to editorial headlines, packaging and interface environments where horizontal space is at a premium. Three weights — Light, Regular and Bold — cover 200 Latin languages with a glyph set of 400 characters.
foundry_blurb: S6 Foundry describe Vertical Sans as a contemporary condensed sans-serif designed around height, compression and strong typographic rhythm. They note its narrow construction allows typography to occupy limited horizontal space without losing clarity, and cite its carefully controlled counters and optical corrections as key to maintaining readability across both large-scale and functional typographic applications.

name: Agger Serif
classification_notes: Agger is a high-contrast serif with large open counters and flowing curves, sitting between classical serif tradition and contemporary proportions, making it a modern display-oriented serif.
summary: A high-contrast contemporary serif with large open counters and flowing curves, aimed at luxury branding, fashion and editorial applications.
description: Agger Serif balances thick-thin stroke contrast with restrained moderation, avoiding ornamentation while maintaining legibility across both editorial and display contexts. Its open counters and curved forms give it a soft, approachable quality that suits beauty, fashion and cultural projects. Five weights run from Light to Bold, covering a range of typographic needs within a single family.
foundry_blurb: S6 Foundry describe Agger Serif as a contemporary high-contrast serif designed with elegance, softness and visual refinement in mind. They note its large open counters and flowing curved forms create a sophisticated yet approachable typographic texture, and position it for luxury branding, beauty, fashion and cultural projects where typography must communicate refinement without excess.

name: Bla Bla
classification_notes: Explicitly designed for display use with exaggerated curves, distorted proportions and dramatic contrast; the foundry describes it as an expressive display serif built to challenge conventional serif restraint.
summary: A five-weight display serif with exaggerated curves, dramatic stroke contrast and large counters aimed at editorial headlines and brand identity work.
description: Built to push against the restraint of conventional serif typography, the family uses distorted proportions, sculpted terminals and flowing strokes to produce something with an irreverent energy. Large counters and dramatic transitions create texture at headline sizes, which is when the expressive character of the letterforms becomes most apparent. Covers 208 Latin languages across 510 glyphs with OpenType feature support.
foundry_blurb: S6 Foundry describe Bla Bla Serif as an expressive display serif built with exaggerated curves, playful rhythm and a strong visual personality, combining classical serif references with distorted proportions to feel both elegant and irreverent. They position it for branding, packaging, editorial headlines and fashion communication where typography is meant to lead rather than support.

name: Aupress
classification_notes: Aupress is classified as a display serif owing to its pronounced thick-to-thin stroke contrast and expressive rhythm, designed explicitly for large-scale typographic applications rather than continuous text.
summary: A high-contrast display serif with sharp structural forms and flowing stroke transitions, aimed at editorial, fashion and branding applications.
description: Built for large-scale typographic use, this contemporary display serif pairs sharp detailing with pronounced thick-to-thin contrast to introduce movement and tension across the letterforms. The design draws from classic editorial typography while adopting more contemporary proportions, keeping it feeling current rather than nostalgic. Three weights cover Light through Bold, with broad Latin language support spanning 785 glyphs.
foundry_blurb: S6 Foundry describe Aupress as a contemporary display typeface combining bold contrast, expressive rhythm and a strong editorial presence. They note that it draws influence from classic editorial typography while introducing contemporary proportions and sharper detailing, and position it for branding, fashion, publishing, packaging and visual identity work where typographic atmosphere and distinction are needed.

name: Hucks Serif
classification_notes: A contemporary serif with defined serifs, sculpted curves and moderated stroke contrast, positioned for editorial and branding use rather than any historical revival tradition.
summary: A contemporary serif with sharp detailing and moderated contrast, aimed at editorial, branding and cultural design work across print and digital.
description: Hucks Serif combines defined serifs, sculpted curves and carefully controlled stroke contrast to produce a stable reading rhythm with a confident editorial character. The family spans five weights from Light to Bold, covering 221 Latin languages across 630 glyphs with OpenType support. It sits comfortably in editorial, publishing, packaging and cultural communication where a clean but authoritative serif voice is required.
foundry_blurb: S6 Foundry describe Hucks Serif as a contemporary serif designed with strong proportions, sharp detailing and a confident editorial presence. They position it as balancing classical structure with modern refinement, suited to editorial design, branding, publishing, packaging and cultural communication where typography needs to communicate depth, precision and contemporary elegance.

name: Magnesia
classification_notes: A contemporary display serif with sharp thick-thin contrast and sculpted letterforms, positioned explicitly as a display and editorial typeface for luxury and fashion contexts.
summary: A high-contrast display serif with sharp stroke transitions and sculpted curves, aimed at luxury branding, fashion and editorial work.
description: Four weights from Thin to Bold give the family a restrained but expressive range, with pronounced thick-thin contrast and carefully controlled proportions intended to produce visual tension without ornament. The foundry positions it for luxury packaging, fashion communication and cultural projects where typography carries atmospheric weight. Coverage extends across 200 Latin-script languages with 450 glyphs and OpenType support.
foundry_blurb: S6 Foundry describe Magnesia as a contemporary display serif designed with sharp contrast, sculpted curves and a strong editorial presence. They say it balances elegant structural forms with dramatic transitions, producing a refined yet highly expressive character suited to luxury branding, fashion communication, editorial design, packaging and cultural projects where typography shapes mood and identity alongside readability.

name: Pulchra
classification_notes: A contemporary serif with classical influences and modern construction, characterised by graceful transitions and balanced contrast typical of editorial-oriented text serifs.
summary: A four-weight contemporary serif with classical proportions and restrained contrast, aimed at editorial, luxury branding and publishing.
description: Pulchra combines classical serif influence with a cleaner modern construction, producing flowing curves and a calm reading rhythm suited to luxury and editorial contexts. Four weights from Light to Bold cover a broad range of typographic needs across print and digital media. With 480 glyphs and support for 190 Latin languages, the family is well-equipped for international publishing and high-end visual identity work.
foundry_blurb: S6 Foundry describe Pulchra as an elegant contemporary serif designed with refined proportions, flowing curves and a calm editorial rhythm. They say it balances sophistication with readability by combining classical serif influence with cleaner modern construction, resulting in typography that feels timeless yet distinctly contemporary. The typeface is positioned for branding, publishing, packaging, cultural communication and high-end visual identities where elegance needs to be communicated with restraint and clarity.

name: Wislet Serif
classification_notes: Wislet Serif combines classical serif structure with softer, fluid contemporary shaping and subtle contrast, placing it in a contemporary serif subcategory with expressive, atmospheric qualities rather than strict historical classification.
summary: A ten-weight contemporary serif by Tyr Wislet with fluid curves and sculpted transitions, aimed at editorial, luxury branding and packaging work.
description: Designed with flowing letterforms and carefully exaggerated details, Wislet Serif draws on classical serif influence while softening contours into a more organic, contemporary rhythm. The family spans ten weights from Thin to Black, offering range for both fine headline work and medium-weight body settings. Coverage extends to 221 Latin languages across 400 glyphs, with OpenType support throughout.
foundry_blurb: S6 Foundry describe Wislet Serif as an expressive contemporary serif built to balance elegance with individuality, combining classical serif influence with softer contemporary shaping and subtle contrast. They position it for editorial design, luxury branding, packaging, fashion communication and cultural projects where typography contributes atmosphere and visual identity alongside readability.

name: Arto Condensed
classification_notes: A narrow display typeface with tall proportions and tightly controlled spacing, explicitly designed for posters, editorial headlines and branding rather than extended text. Its compressed construction and sharp detailing place it firmly in the condensed display category.
summary: A narrow display typeface with tall proportions and sharp detailing, built for posters, editorial headlines and space-constrained typographic work.
description: Arto Condensed offers five weights from Light to Bold, each sharing the same compressed construction that lets large text blocks occupy minimal horizontal space without sacrificing legibility. Carefully balanced counters and disciplined proportions keep the letterforms readable at scale, while clean transitions give the family a contemporary, graphic tone. The range suits posters, packaging, branding and motion graphics where a strong vertical rhythm is required.
foundry_blurb: S6 Foundry describe Arto Condensed as a narrow display typeface built for impact, compression and strong vertical rhythm. They highlight its tall proportions and tightly controlled spacing as tools for creating bold typographic compositions while maintaining clarity, and position the family across posters, branding, editorial headlines, packaging and motion graphics. With 201 Latin language supports and 410 glyphs, the family is designed for environments where typography needs to feel immediate, modern and space-efficient.

name: Supernova
classification_notes: A high-energy display typeface built on sharp angular geometry and expanded proportions, placing it firmly in the display category with a geometric, futuristic character rather than any conventional text classification.
summary: A sharp-edged geometric display family in three weights, built for large-scale poster and motion graphics work with expanded proportions and high visual contrast.
description: Three weights from Light to Medium cover the essentials for display-only use, with no italic or condensed cuts available. Angular construction and exaggerated spacing give compositions an expansive, cinematic quality suited to entertainment branding, music visuals and experimental identity systems. Coverage spans over 200 Latin languages with 420 glyphs and OpenType support.
foundry_blurb: S6 Foundry describe Supernova as a futuristic display typeface combining sharp geometry and expanded proportions with dynamic rhythm to produce typography that feels experimental, cinematic and distinctly contemporary. They position it for posters, motion graphics, entertainment branding, digital art direction and experimental identity systems where typography is intended to dominate the composition and create atmosphere.

name: Bix Metric
classification_notes: Described as a modular, geometric display typeface with mechanical rhythm and architectural spacing, explicitly designed for large-scale expressive compositions rather than text setting.
summary: A modular geometric display family from S6 Foundry, built around rigid structural forms and mechanical rhythm for posters, branding and experimental work.
description: Five weights from Light to Bold share an architecture of sharp geometric construction, unconventional proportions and strong graphic repetition. The family is not intended for small sizes; it is built for compositions where type acts as a dominant graphic element, lending itself to posters, motion graphics, packaging and identity systems with an industrial or experimental tone. Coverage extends to 208 Latin languages across 510 glyphs.
foundry_blurb: S6 Foundry describe Bix Metric as an experimental display typeface built around modular geometry, sharp construction and strong graphic rhythm. They position it as a display system designed for immediate visual impact, with a mechanical rhythm suited to posters, branding systems, motion graphics, packaging and visual identities requiring an experimental or industrial tone.

name: Hupla
classification_notes: Explicitly positioned as a display typeface with playful proportions, exaggerated rhythm and bold structural forms combined with rounded details, placing it firmly in the expressive display category rather than a neutral text or serif classification.
summary: A playful display typeface with exaggerated proportions and rounded structural forms, intended for branding, packaging and editorial headlines.
description: Built around irregular rhythms, curved transitions and dynamic spacing, Hupla aims to feel lively and spontaneous whilst staying visually controlled. Five weights from Light to Bold serve headline and display settings in branding, packaging, motion graphics and cultural communication. The typeface covers 221 Latin languages across 720 glyphs with OpenType support.
foundry_blurb: S6 Foundry describe Hupla as an expressive display typeface with playful proportions, exaggerated rhythm and a strong sense of movement. They note it combines bold structural forms with softer rounded details to feel energetic and unconventional, and recommend it for branding, packaging, editorial headlines, motion graphics and cultural communication where typography is meant to create atmosphere and individuality.

name: Faffin Display
classification_notes: Classified as display due to its exaggerated proportions, dramatic contrast and sharp detailing explicitly designed for large-scale, high-impact use rather than text setting. The geometric structure with playful distortion places it within the display sans category.
summary: A high-contrast display typeface with exaggerated proportions and sharp detailing, offered in five named weights with variable font support across 221 Latin languages.
description: Built around structured geometry with deliberate distortion, the family produces a distinctive typographic rhythm suited to large-scale work. Five weights from Light to Bold are available as a variable font, giving designers fine control over weight across posters, packaging and editorial headlines. The 720-glyph set covers 221 Latin languages, making it broadly usable across multilingual design projects.
foundry_blurb: S6 Foundry describe Faffin Display as a bold contemporary display typeface built for large-scale typography and strong visual expression. They note its exaggerated proportions, sharp detailing and dramatic contrast, and position the family for posters, branding, packaging, editorial headlines, motion graphics and cultural communication where typography must carry atmosphere and identity across print and digital media.

name: Somtam
classification_notes: Explicitly described as a display typeface with exaggerated proportions, playful rhythm and sharp structural contrasts, placing it firmly in the expressive display category rather than any neutral classification.
summary: A four-weight expressive display family built around exaggerated proportions and sharp structural contrasts, aimed at branding, packaging and editorial headlines.
description: Soft curves sit against hard structural transitions to give the family a restless, energetic quality suited to cultural communication, music visuals and creative campaigns. Dynamic spacing and sculpted letterforms create compositions rich in character while staying visually controlled. The family covers over 200 Latin languages across 430 glyphs with OpenType support.
foundry_blurb: S6 Foundry describe Somtam as an expressive display typeface with playful rhythm, exaggerated proportions and a bold contemporary attitude. They note that it combines soft curves with sharp structural contrasts to feel energetic and unconventional, and recommend it for branding, packaging, editorial headlines, cultural communication, music visuals and creative campaigns where typography needs to carry emotion and individuality.

name: Olap Metric
classification_notes: Olap Metric is a modular, geometric display typeface with angular, compressed forms and a mechanical visual character, placing it firmly in the geometric display category rather than a conventional sans-serif classification.
summary: A modular geometric display typeface built around angular forms and compressed geometry for posters, motion graphics and experimental branding.
description: Rigid structural construction and unconventional proportions give this family a mechanical, futuristic character suited to large-scale and visually aggressive layouts. The system-driven approach treats alignment, spacing and repetition as aesthetic elements in themselves, producing a consistent graphic rhythm across compositions. Available in 9 styles with coverage of 180 Latin languages, it targets art direction and visual identity work where type is intended to dominate rather than recede.
foundry_blurb: S6 Foundry describe Olap Metric as an experimental display typeface built on modular construction, geometric repetition and sharp graphic rhythm, designed to produce strong visual impact through rigid structural forms and unconventional proportions. They position it for posters, motion graphics, branding, packaging, digital art direction and experimental visual identities where typography is meant to dominate the composition, citing its angular transitions and compressed geometry as giving it a mechanical energy suited to large-scale and visually aggressive layouts.
